description_long: This is a weathered rectangular stone block with a rough, uneven surface and visible signs of age, such as chips and discoloration. The block is primarily a reddish-brown color, with darker patches and some lighter worn areas, suggesting exposure to the elements. One face of the block features engraved text reading 'PEEBLES BLOCK PORTSMOUTH,' indicating its origin or manufacturer. The edges and corners are irregular and slightly rounded, likely from prolonged use or environmental wear. The block appears solid and dense, typical of masonry used in urban construction or historical sites. Its overall appearance suggests it was once part of a larger structure or used as a boundary or obstacle in an urban setting.
description: A weathered, reddish-brown rectangular stone block with engraved text and rough, chipped surfaces. It shows signs of age and urban use.
description_view_0: The front face displays the engraved text 'PEEBLES BLOCK PORTSMOUTH' and highlights the rough, chipped surface texture.
description_view_1: This side view shows the block's elongated shape and the worn, darkened surface with minimal visible markings.
description_view_2: The underside and side are visible, showing a more uniform, flat surface with some cracks and less pronounced weathering.
description_view_3: The end of the block is shown, revealing rounded, chipped corners and a pitted, rough texture.
attribute_location_context: historic urban sidewalk or boundary marker
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set in compacted earth
attribute_weathering_pattern: The block shows chipped corners, surface pitting, and discoloration from prolonged outdoor exposure. There are no signs of moss or biological growth.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Engraved text is present, reading 'PEEBLES BLOCK PORTSMOUTH' on one face.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely forms part of a continuous row or boundary with similar blocks.
